lesson.menu_desc: Putting Block 3 together in one global sync -- 27 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 15 -- Review -- A Global Product Sync
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Patrícia Xavier Lima Iwashita | Aula 15 -- Review -- A Global Product Sync

listenings.text: Hi Patrícia, this is David from the product team in San Francisco. I am leaving a quick note before our global sync tomorrow. Here is my plan for the call. First, I will walk the team through the roadmap, so everyone is aligned on the next release. We are launching the new reporting feature in May, and I am going to share the timeline for the rest of the quarter. I know the time zones are tricky, so I will keep my part short. One small piece of feedback from my side: the last deck had a lot of detail, and perhaps it might be better to start with the big picture. Could you bring the client's top requests to the sync? Let us circle back on the training dates after we agree on the roadmap. Thanks, and talk tomorrow.

Hi Patrícia, it is Priya in Bangalore, just a short note before the product sync. I have read David's roadmap, and it looks good from my side. In our last call, the team said the new feature would ship in May, and that is still the plan. I want to build a little rapport with the new engineers, so I will start with a quick hello before we get into the details. One takeaway from the client feedback: they really like the new dashboard, but they asked for fewer clicks. I told them we would look at it, so it is now an action item, and I am the owner. The time zone overlap is small, so let us keep the sync focused. If we run out of time, we can circle back by email. See you on the call.
